components.title: Email Security Policy
components.description: Defines requirements for securing email communications, including anti-phishing controls, attachment and link scanning, email encryption, and acceptable use obligations for all email system users. Mandates SPF, DKIM, and DMARC configuration for all sending domains.
components.purpose: Establish and enforce email security controls to protect against phishing and malware.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Email Security Policy.

implemented-requirements.description: The email security policy requires email content filtering capable of detecting and blocking malicious attachments and links, including sandbox detonation of attachments before delivery to end users.

The email security policy restricts execution of active content (scripts, macros) delivered via email. Attachments containing active content must be evaluated by an email scanning solution before delivery.
The email security policy requires that email client security settings are hardened and locked, preventing users from bypassing scanning or enabling unsafe content rendering in email messages.

components.title: Microsoft Defender for Office 365 — Email Filtering
components.description: Microsoft Defender for Office 365 Plan 2 provides advanced email threat protection for the Microsoft 365 Exchange Online tenant. Provides Safe Attachments (sandbox detonation), Safe Links (URL rewriting and click-time scanning), anti-phishing (impersonation detection), and anti-spam policies. Configured for the Australian Government Community Cloud tenant.
components.purpose: Scan and filter inbound and outbound email for malware, phishing, and malicious links.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Microsoft Defender for Office 365 — Email Filtering.

implemented-requirements.description: Defender for Office 365 Safe Attachments detonates all email attachments in a cloud sandbox before delivery. Malicious attachments are blocked and quarantined. Zero-hour auto purge (ZAP) retroactively removes malware delivered before signature updates were available.

Safe Links rewrites and re-checks all URLs in emails at time of click, blocking navigation to known malicious sites even if the link was clean at delivery time. URL detonation analyses suspicious links in a sandbox before the user's browser opens them.

Defender for Office 365 anti-phishing policy uses mailbox intelligence, impersonation protection for executive accounts, and spoof intelligence to detect and quarantine phishing attempts. DMARC, DKIM, and SPF are enforced for all outbound sending domains.

components.title: Microsoft Outlook — Email Client
components.description: Microsoft Outlook (as part of Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise) is the approved email client on all Windows 11 workstations. Security settings are locked via Group Policy and Intune: macros are disabled, external content loading is blocked, and automatic preview of potentially dangerous attachment types is disabled.
components.purpose: Provide a secure, hardened email client for all organisational users.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Microsoft Outlook — Email Client.

implemented-requirements.description: Outlook security settings are enforced via Group Policy (ADMX templates): external image loading requires user confirmation, automatic download of external content in HTML email is disabled, attachment preview for executable file types is disabled, and S/MIME is configured for encrypted and digitally signed email where required.

Outlook is configured to block active content in email: macros embedded in Office documents attached to email are blocked; JavaScript and VBScript execution in Outlook forms is disabled; automatic execution of email-delivered scripts is prevented via restricted attachment type rules.

Outlook is integrated with Defender for Office 365: the Report Message add-in is deployed to all users for user-reported phishing; Defender protection applies to all mail rendered in Outlook clients via the Microsoft 365 ATP connector.

capabilities.name: Email Security
capabilities.description: The email security capability combines policy, cloud-based threat filtering, and hardened client configuration to protect against phishing, malware, and malicious links delivered via email. Microsoft Defender for Office 365 provides attachment sandboxing and URL scanning; Outlook configuration blocks active content; the email security policy establishes governance and anti-spoofing requirements.

incorporates-components.description: Defines email security policy requirements including SPF/DKIM/DMARC obligations.
Provides cloud-based email scanning, attachment sandboxing, and URL rewriting.
Provides a hardened email client with active content and external image loading blocked.

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for capability: Email Security. Includes contributions from member components (identified by source-component-uuid prop) and capability-level synthesis where present.

implemented-requirements.description: Defender for Office 365 Safe Attachments sandboxes all attachments; Outlook is integrated with Defender reporting; the email security policy mandates these controls.
Safe Links blocks malicious URLs at click time; Outlook blocks active content execution; the email security policy prohibits scripts delivered via email.
Anti-phishing impersonation detection and DMARC/DKIM/SPF enforcement are provided by Defender for Office 365; Outlook ADMX settings lock client-side security configuration.
